meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M104 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M104 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M104 is a 2.8–3.2-litre straight-six — one of Mercedes' most revered petrol engines, but its wiring harness and head gasket are known weak points. Backed by real UK enquiries from EnginesMarket.co.uk.

rows.metric: Confidence: 82% confidence — based on verified specialist rebuild reports and UK workshop consensus.

rows.value: Mix of cheap fixes (gaskets, sensors) and expensive ones (head gasket, wiring harness)

rows.metric: Available but dealer/specialist-order only; some parts becoming scarce

rows.value: Recon cost 30–44% of lowest host-vehicle value

One-line verdict: The M104 is a smooth, characterful straight-six and a modern classic — but biodegradable wiring harnesses and head gaskets are its defining weaknesses — budget for preventative replacement or restoration.

rows.metric: Best for: Enthusiasts wanting a classic Mercedes straight-six experience with strong performance.
rows.value: Avoid if: You're not prepared for classic-car maintenance costs or wiring harness replacement.

rows.metric: Labour estimate: Fitting an M104 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).
rows.value: → Compare M104 engine prices from UK specialists
1. How much does an M104 engine replacement cost?

rows.metric: A reconditioned M104 typically costs £2,400–£3,800 supply only, varying by displacement and specific variant. Fitting adds £900–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.
rows.value: 2. Is the Mercedes M104 a reliable engine?

rows.metric: The M104 is rated 3.5★ out of 5 — it is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' best straight-six engines, with a characterful, smooth power delivery and strong durability. However, the biodegradable wiring harness and head gasket issues are well-documented weak points that require attention.
rows.value: 3. What is the most common M104 failure?

rows.metric: The most common issues are biodegradable wiring harness failure (the insulation degrades, causing electrical faults) and head gasket failure (especially on 3.2-litre variants). Camshaft wear and coil pack failures are also reported on higher-mileage examples.
rows.value: 4. Which Mercedes models use the M104?

rows.metric: The M104 powered a wide range of Mercedes models including the E-Class (W124/W210), S-Class (W140), SL-Class (R129), C-Class (W202), CLK-Class (C208), and G-Class (W463), across 2.8-litre and 3.2-litre displacements.
rows.value: 5. How long does the M104 last?

rows.metric: With proper maintenance — including wiring harness replacement if original and head gasket monitoring — the M104 is capable of 200,000+ miles. Many examples remain on UK roads today, testament to its fundamental engineering quality.
